body.description: The raw `JSON` body of the response.

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the storage pool

name.description: Identifier. Name of the storage pool

type.description: Optional. Type of the storage pool. This field is used to control whether the pool supports `FILE` based volumes only or `UNIFIED` (both `FILE` and `BLOCK`) volumes. If not specified during creation, it defaults to `FILE`.
enumDescriptions: Storage pool type is not specified.

qosType.description: Optional. QoS (Quality of Service) Type of the storage pool

availableThroughputMibps.description: Output only. Available throughput of the storage pool (in MiB/s).

enableHotTierAutoResize.description: Optional. Flag indicating that the hot-tier threshold will be auto-increased by 10% of the hot-tier when it hits 100%. Default is true. The increment will kick in only if the new size after increment is still less than or equal to storage pool size.

totalIops.description: Optional. Custom Performance Total IOPS of the pool if not provided, it will be calculated based on the total_throughput_mibps

description.description: Optional. Description of the storage pool

labels.description: Optional. Labels as key value pairs

capacityGib.description: Required. Capacity in GIB of the pool

createTime.description: Output only. Create time of the storage pool

customPerformanceEnabled.description: Optional. True if using Independent Scaling of capacity and performance (Hyperdisk) By default set to false

state.description: Output only. State of the storage pool

hotTierSizeUsedGib.description: Output only. Total hot tier data rounded down to the nearest GiB used by the storage pool.

activeDirectory.description: Optional. Specifies the Active Directory to be used for creating a SMB volume.

satisfiesPzi.description: Output only. Reserved for future use

encryptionType.description: Output only. Specifies the current pool encryption key source.
enumDescriptions: The source of the encryption key is not specified.
Customer managed encryption key, which is stored in KMS.

satisfiesPzs.description: Output only. Reserved for future use

hotTierSizeGib.description: Optional. Total hot tier capacity for the Storage Pool. It is applicable only to Flex service level. It should be less than the minimum storage pool size and cannot be more than the current storage pool size. It cannot be decreased once set.

psaRange.description: Optional. This field is not implemented. The values provided in this field are ignored.

serviceLevel.description: Required. Service level of the storage pool

mode.description: Optional. Mode of the storage pool. This field is used to control whether the user can perform ONTAP operations on the storage pool using the GCNV ONTAP Mode APIs. If not specified during creation, it defaults to `DEFAULT`.
enumDescriptions: The resource is managed by the GCNV APIs.
The resource is managed by the GCNV ONTAP Mode APIs.

zone.description: Optional. Specifies the active zone for regional storagePool.

scaleType.description: Optional. The scale type of the storage pool. Defaults to `SCALE_TYPE_DEFAULT` if not specified.
enumDescriptions: Represents standard capacity and performance scale-type. Suitable for general purpose workloads.
Represents higher capacity and performance scale-type. Suitable for more demanding workloads.

coldTierSizeUsedGib.description: Output only. Total cold tier data rounded down to the nearest GiB used by the storage pool.

volumeCount.description: Output only. Volume count of the storage pool

kmsConfig.description: Optional. Specifies the KMS config to be used for volume encryption.

globalAccessAllowed.description: Deprecated. Used to allow SO pool to access AD or DNS server from other regions.

replicaZone.description: Optional. Specifies the replica zone for regional storagePool.

totalThroughputMibps.description: Optional. Custom Performance Total Throughput of the pool (in MiBps)

ldapEnabled.description: Optional. Flag indicating if the pool is NFS LDAP enabled or not.

volumeCapacityGib.description: Output only. Allocated size of all volumes in GIB in the storage pool

allowAutoTiering.description: Optional. True if the storage pool supports Auto Tiering enabled volumes. Default is false. Auto-tiering can be enabled after storage pool creation but it can't be disabled once enabled.

StoragePool.description: StoragePool is a container for volumes with a service level and capacity. Volumes can be created in a pool of sufficient available capacity. StoragePool capacity is what you are billed for.

description.description: A description of the snapshot with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

createTime.description: Output only. The time when the snapshot was created.

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of the snapshot. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}`.

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the storage pool

usedBytes.description: Output only. Current storage usage for the snapshot in bytes.

labels.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

Snapshot.description: Snapshot is a point-in-time version of a Volume's content.

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details. You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

state.description: Output only. State of the host group.

createTime.description: Output only. Create time of the host group.

osType.description: Required. The OS type of the host group. It indicates the type of operating system used by all of the hosts in the HostGroup. All hosts in a HostGroup must be of the same OS type. This can be set only when creating a HostGroup.

description.description: Optional. Description of the host group.

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of the host group. Format: `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/hostGroups/{host_group_id}`.

type.description: Required. Type of the host group.

hosts.description: Required. The list of hosts associated with the host group.

labels.description: Optional. Labels of the host group.

HostGroup.description: Host group is a collection of hosts that can be used for accessing a Block Volume.

backupVaults.description: A list of backupVaults in the project for the specified location.

shareName.description: Destination volume's share name. If not specified, source volume's share name will be used.

tieringPolicy.description: Optional. Tiering policy for the volume.

volumeId.description: Desired destination volume resource id. If not specified, source volume's resource id will be used. This value must start with a lowercase letter followed by up to 62 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens, and cannot end with a hyphen.

DestinationVolumeParameters.description: DestinationVolumeParameters specify input parameters used for creating destination volume.

locations.description: A list of locations that matches the specified filter in the request.

SyncReplicationRequest.description: SyncReplicationRequest syncs the replication from source to destination.

ListSnapshotsResponse.description: ListSnapshotsResponse is the result of ListSnapshotsRequest.

snapshots.description: A list of snapshots in the project for the specified volume.

nextPageToken.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

HybridPeeringDetails.description: HybridPeeringDetails contains details about the hybrid peering.

command.description: Output only. Copy-paste-able commands to be used on user's ONTAP to accept peering requests.

peerClusterName.description: Output only. Name of the user's local source cluster to be peered with the destination cluster.

commandExpiryTime.description: Output only. Expiration time for the peering command to be executed on user's ONTAP.

subnetIp.description: Output only. IP address of the subnet.

passphrase.description: Output only. Temporary passphrase generated to accept cluster peering command.

peerVolumeName.description: Output only. Name of the user's local source volume to be peered with the destination volume.

peerSvmName.description: Output only. Name of the user's local source vserver svm to be peered with the destination vserver svm.

hostGroups.description: Optional. A list of host groups that identify hosts that can mount the block volume. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/hostGroups/{host_group_id}` This field can be updated after the block device is created.

sizeGib.description: Optional. The size of the block device in GiB. Any value provided for the `size_gib` field during volume creation is ignored. The block device's size is system-managed and will be set to match the parent Volume's `capacity_gib`.

identifier.description: Output only. Device identifier of the block volume. This represents `lun_serial_number` for iSCSI volumes.

name.description: Optional. User-defined name for the block device, unique within the volume. In case no user input is provided, name will be auto-generated in the backend. The name must meet the following requirements: * Be between 1 and 255 characters long. * Contain only uppercase or lowercase letters (A-Z, a-z), numbers (0-9), and the following special characters: "-", "_", "}", "{", ".". * Spaces are not allowed.

osType.description: Required. Immutable. The OS type of the volume. This field can't be changed after the block device is created.

BlockDevice.description: Block device represents the device(s) which are stored in the block volume.

GoogleProtobufEmpty.description: A generic empty message that you can re-use to avoid defining duplicated empty messages in your APIs. A typical example is to use it as the request or the response type of an API method. For instance: service Foo { rpc Bar(google.protobuf.Empty) returns (google.protobuf.Empty); }

ldapEnabled.description: Output only. Flag indicating if the volume is NFS LDAP enabled or not.

hasReplication.description: Output only. Indicates whether the volume is part of a replication relationship.

unixPermissions.description: Optional. Default unix style permission (e.g. 777) the mount point will be created with. Applicable for NFS protocol types only.

replicaZone.description: Output only. Specifies the replica zone for regional volume.

smbSettings.description: Optional. SMB share settings for the volume.

enumDescriptions: SMB setting not to notify change
SMB setting to show previous versions
SMB setting to access volume based on enumerartion

snapReserve.description: Optional. Snap_reserve specifies percentage of volume storage reserved for snapshot storage. Default is 0 percent.

kmsConfig.description: Output only. Specifies the KMS config to be used for volume encryption.

zone.description: Output only. Specifies the active zone for regional volume.

multipleEndpoints.description: Optional. Flag indicating if the volume will have an IP address per node for volumes supporting multiple IP endpoints. Only the volume with large_capacity will be allowed to have multiple endpoints.

serviceLevel.description: Output only. Service level of the volume

usedGib.description: Output only. Used capacity in GIB of the volume. This is computed periodically and it does not represent the realtime usage.

exportPolicy.description: Optional. Export policy of the volume

activeDirectory.description: Output only. Specifies the ActiveDirectory name of a SMB volume.

psaRange.description: Output only. This field is not implemented. The values provided in this field are ignored.

blockDevices.description: Optional. Block devices for the volume. Currently, only one block device is permitted per Volume.

restrictedActions.description: Optional. List of actions that are restricted on this volume.

enumDescriptions: Prevent volume from being deleted when mounted.

encryptionType.description: Output only. Specified the current volume encryption key source.
enumDescriptions: The source of the encryption key is not specified.
Customer managed encryption key, which is stored in KMS.

hotTierSizeUsedGib.description: Output only. Total hot tier data rounded down to the nearest GiB used by the Volume. This field is only used for flex Service Level

cloneDetails.description: Output only. If this volume is a clone, this field contains details about the clone.

state.description: Output only. State of the volume
enumDescriptions: Volume State is Preparing. Note that this is different from CREATING where CREATING means the volume is being created, while PREPARING means the volume is created and now being prepared for the replication.

snapshotDirectory.description: Optional. Snapshot_directory if enabled (true) the volume will contain a read-only .snapshot directory which provides access to each of the volume's snapshots.

restoreParameters.description: Optional. Specifies the source of the volume to be created from.

protocols.description: Required. Protocols required for the volume

storagePool.description: Required. StoragePool name of the volume

labels.description: Optional. Labels as key value pairs

mountOptions.description: Output only. Mount options of this volume

throughputMibps.description: Optional. Throughput of the volume (in MiB/s)

network.description: Output only. VPC Network name. Format: projects/{project}/global/networks/{network}

createTime.description: Output only. Create time of the volume

capacityGib.description: Required. Capacity in GIB of the volume

cacheParameters.description: Optional. Cache parameters for the volume.

largeCapacity.description: Optional. Flag indicating if the volume will be a large capacity volume or a regular volume. This field is used for legacy FILE pools. For Unified pools, use the `large_capacity_config` field instead. This field and `large_capacity_config` are mutually exclusive.

coldTierSizeGib.description: Output only. Size of the volume cold tier data rounded down to the nearest GiB.

hybridReplicationParameters.description: Optional. The Hybrid Replication parameters for the volume.

kerberosEnabled.description: Optional. Flag indicating if the volume is a kerberos volume or not, export policy rules control kerberos security modes (krb5, krb5i, krb5p).

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the volume

largeCapacityConfig.description: Optional. Large capacity config for the volume. Enables and configures large capacity for volumes in Unified pools with File protocols. Not applicable for Block protocols in Unified pools. This field and the legacy `large_capacity` boolean field are mutually exclusive.

securityStyle.description: Optional. Security Style of the Volume

name.description: Identifier. Name of the volume

shareName.description: Required. Share name of the volume

Volume.description: Volume provides a filesystem that you can mount.

KmsConfig.description: KmsConfig is the customer-managed encryption key(CMEK) configuration.

state.description: Output only. State of the KmsConfig.
enumDescriptions: KmsConfig State is Pending to verify crypto key access.
KmsConfig State is Not accessbile by the SDE service account to the crypto key.
KmsConfig State is Migrating. The existing volumes are migrating from SMEK to CMEK.

instructions.description: Output only. Instructions to provide the access to the customer provided encryption key.

cryptoKeyName.description: Required. Customer-managed crypto key resource full name. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/keyRings/{key_ring}/cryptoKeys/{crypto_key}`

name.description: Identifier. Name of the `KmsConfig`. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/kmsConfigs/{kms_config}`

createTime.description: Output only. Create time of the KmsConfig.

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the KmsConfig.

serviceAccount.description: Output only. The Service account which will have access to the customer provided encryption key.

SwitchActiveReplicaZoneRequest.description: SwitchActiveReplicaZoneRequest switch the active/replica zone for a regional storagePool.

operations.description: A list of operations that matches the specified filter in the request.

unreachable.description: Unordered list. Unreachable resources. Populated when the request sets `ListOperationsRequest.return_partial_success` and reads across collections. For example, when attempting to list all resources across all supported locations.

TieringPolicy.description: Defines tiering policy for the volume.

tierAction.description: Optional. Flag indicating if the volume has tiering policy enable/pause. Default is PAUSED.
enumDescriptions: When tiering is enabled, new cold data will be tiered.
When paused, tiering won't be performed on new data. Existing data stays tiered until accessed.

coolingThresholdDays.description: Optional. Time in days to mark the volume's data block as cold and make it eligible for tiering, can be range from 2-183. Default is 31.

hotTierBypassModeEnabled.description: Optional. Flag indicating that the hot tier bypass mode is enabled. Default is false. This is only applicable to Flex service level.

StopReplicationRequest.description: StopReplicationRequest stops a replication until resumed.

force.description: Indicates whether to stop replication forcefully while data transfer is in progress. Warning! if force is true, this will abort any current transfers and can lead to data loss due to partial transfer. If force is false, stop replication will fail while data transfer is in progress and you will need to retry later.

directoryServiceType.description: Type of directory service policy attached to the storage pool.
enumDescriptions: Directory service type is not specified.
Active directory policy attached to the storage pool.

ValidateDirectoryServiceRequest.description: ValidateDirectoryServiceRequest validates the directory service policy attached to the storage pool.

RestoreBackupFilesRequest.description: RestoreBackupFilesRequest restores files from a backup to a volume.

backup.description: Required. The backup resource name, in the format `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/backupVaults/{backup_vault_id}/backups/{backup_id}`

fileList.description: Required. List of files to be restored, specified by their absolute path in the source volume.

restoreDestinationPath.description: Optional. Absolute directory path in the destination volume. This is required if the `file_list` is provided.

EncryptVolumesRequest.description: EncryptVolumesRequest specifies the KMS config to encrypt existing volumes.

minute.description: Set the minute of the hour to start the snapshot (0-59), defaults to the top of the hour (0).

hour.description: Set the hour to start the snapshot (0-23), defaults to midnight (0).

daysOfMonth.description: Set the day or days of the month to make a snapshot (1-31). Accepts a comma separated number of days. Defaults to '1'.

snapshotsToKeep.description: The maximum number of Snapshots to keep for the hourly schedule

MonthlySchedule.description: Make a snapshot once a month e.g. at 2nd 04:00, 7th 05:20, 24th 23:50

ListBackupPoliciesResponse.description: ListBackupPoliciesResponse contains all the backup policies requested.

nextPageToken.description: A token identifying a page of results the server should return.

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

ListBackupsResponse.description: ListBackupsResponse is the result of ListBackupsRequest.

backups.description: A list of backups in the project.

nextPageToken.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

excludePathList.description: Optional. List of directory-paths to be excluded for pre-population for the FlexCache volume.

recursion.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether the directories listed with the `path_list` need to be recursively pre-populated.

pathList.description: Optional. List of directory-paths to be pre-populated for the FlexCache volume.

CachePrePopulate.description: Pre-populate cache volume with data from the origin volume.

EstablishVolumePeeringRequest.description: EstablishVolumePeeringRequest establishes cluster and svm peerings between the source and destination clusters.

peerIpAddresses.description: Optional. List of IPv4 IP addresses to be used for peering.

peerSvmName.description: Required. Name of the user's local source vserver svm to be peered with the destination vserver svm.

peerVolumeName.description: Required. Name of the user's local source volume to be peered with the destination volume.

peerClusterName.description: Required. Name of the user's local source cluster to be peered with the destination cluster.

role.description: Output only. Indicates whether this points to source or destination.

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the replication.

clusterLocation.description: Optional. Location of the user cluster.

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of the Replication. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}/replications/{replication_id}`.

destinationVolumeParameters.description: Required. Input only. Destination volume parameters

sourceVolume.description: Output only. Full name of source volume resource. Example : "projects/{project}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}"

mirrorState.description: Output only. Indicates the state of mirroring.
enumDescriptions: Destination volume has been initialized and is ready to receive replication transfers.
Destination volume is not receiving replication transfers.
Replication is being managed from Onprem ONTAP.
Peering is yet to be established.

destinationVolume.description: Output only. Full name of destination volume resource. Example : "projects/{project}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}"

description.description: A description about this replication relationship.

labels.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

hybridReplicationUserCommands.description: Output only. Copy pastable snapmirror commands to be executed on onprem cluster by the customer.

hybridReplicationType.description: Output only. Type of the hybrid replication.
enumDescriptions: Hybrid replication type for continuous replication.
New field for reversible OnPrem replication, to be used for data protection.
Hybrid replication type for incremental Transfer in the reverse direction (GCNV is source and Onprem is destination)

healthy.description: Output only. Condition of the relationship. Can be one of the following: - true: The replication relationship is healthy. It has not missed the most recent scheduled transfer. - false: The replication relationship is not healthy. It has missed the most recent scheduled transfer.

state.description: Output only. State of the replication.
enumDescriptions: Replication is waiting for cluster peering to be established.
Replication is waiting for SVM peering to be established.
Replication is waiting for Commands to be executed on Onprem ONTAP.
Onprem ONTAP is destination and Replication can only be managed from Onprem.

replicationSchedule.description: Required. Indicates the schedule for replication.

Replication.description: Replication is a nested resource under Volume, that describes a cross-region replication relationship between 2 volumes in different regions.

BackupConfig.description: BackupConfig contains backup related config on a volume.

backupVault.description: Optional. Name of backup vault. Format: projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/backupVaults/{backup_vault_id}

scheduledBackupEnabled.description: Optional. When set to true, scheduled backup is enabled on the volume. This field should be nil when there's no backup policy attached.

backupChainBytes.description: Output only. Total size of all backups in a chain in bytes = baseline backup size + sum(incremental backup size).

backupPolicies.description: Optional. When specified, schedule backups will be created based on the policy configuration.

RestoreParameters.description: The RestoreParameters if volume is created from a snapshot or backup.

sourceSnapshot.description: Full name of the snapshot resource. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume}/snapshots/{snapshot}

sourceBackup.description: Full name of the backup resource. Format for standard backup: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/backupVaults/{backup_vault_id}/backups/{backup_id}. Format for BackupDR backup: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/backupVaults/{backup_vault}/dataSources/{data_source}/backups/{backup}

body.description: Required. The raw `JSON` body of the request. The body should be in the format of the ONTAP resource. For example: ``` { "body": { "field1": "value1", "field2": "value2", } } ```

peerVolumeName.description: Required. Name of the origin volume for the cache volume.

peerSvmName.description: Required. Name of the origin volume's SVM.

passphrase.description: Output only. Temporary passphrase generated to accept cluster peering command.

stateDetails.description: Output only. Detailed description of the current cache state.

peeringCommandExpiryTime.description: Optional. Expiration time for the peering command to be executed on user's ONTAP.

peerClusterName.description: Required. Name of the origin volume's ONTAP cluster.

cacheConfig.description: Optional. Configuration of the cache volume.

peerIpAddresses.description: Required. List of IC LIF addresses of the origin volume's ONTAP cluster.

enableGlobalFileLock.description: Optional. Indicates whether the cache volume has global file lock enabled.

cacheState.description: Output only. State of the cache volume indicating the peering status.
enumDescriptions: State indicating waiting for cluster peering to be established.
State indicating waiting for SVM peering to be established.
State indicating successful establishment of peering with origin volumes's ONTAP cluster.
Terminal state wherein peering with origin volume's ONTAP cluster has failed.

command.description: Output only. Copy-paste-able commands to be used on user's ONTAP to accept peering requests.

VerifyKmsConfigResponse.description: VerifyKmsConfigResponse contains the information if the config is correctly and error message.

healthy.description: Output only. If the customer key configured correctly to the encrypt volume.

instructions.description: Output only. Instructions for the customers to provide the access to the encryption key.

healthError.description: Output only. Error message if config is not healthy.

supportedFlexPerformance.description: Output only. Supported flex performance in a location.

enumDescriptions: Flex Storage Pool with default performance.
Flex Storage Pool with custom performance.

hasVcp.description: Output only. Indicates if the location has VCP support.

hasOntapProxy.description: Output only. Indicates if the location has ONTAP Proxy support.

supportedServiceLevels.description: Output only. Supported service levels in a location.

nextPageToken.description: A token identifying a page of results the server should return.

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

ListHostGroupsResponse.description: ListHostGroupsResponse is the response to a ListHostGroupsRequest.

body.description: The raw `JSON` body of the response.

weeklyBackupImmutable.description: Optional. Indicates if the weekly backups are immutable. At least one of daily_backup_immutable, weekly_backup_immutable, monthly_backup_immutable and manual_backup_immutable must be true.

dailyBackupImmutable.description: Optional. Indicates if the daily backups are immutable. At least one of daily_backup_immutable, weekly_backup_immutable, monthly_backup_immutable and manual_backup_immutable must be true.

manualBackupImmutable.description: Optional. Indicates if the manual backups are immutable. At least one of daily_backup_immutable, weekly_backup_immutable, monthly_backup_immutable and manual_backup_immutable must be true.

backupMinimumEnforcedRetentionDays.description: Required. Minimum retention duration in days for backups in the backup vault.

monthlyBackupImmutable.description: Optional. Indicates if the monthly backups are immutable. At least one of daily_backup_immutable, weekly_backup_immutable, monthly_backup_immutable and manual_backup_immutable must be true.

BackupRetentionPolicy.description: Retention policy for backups in the backup vault

ExportPolicy.description: Defines the export policy for the volume.

rules.description: Required. List of export policy rules

ReverseReplicationDirectionRequest.description: ReverseReplicationDirectionRequest reverses direction of replication. Source becomes destination and destination becomes source.

cachePrePopulate.description: Optional. Pre-populate cache volume with data from the origin volume.

writebackEnabled.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether writeback is enabled for the FlexCache volume.

cachePrePopulateState.description: Output only. State of the prepopulation job indicating how the prepopulation is progressing.
enumDescriptions: State representing when the most recent create or update request did not require a prepopulation job.
State representing when the most recent update request requested a prepopulation job but it has not yet completed.
State representing when the most recent update request requested a prepopulation job and it has completed successfully.
State representing when the most recent update request requested a prepopulation job but the prepopulate job failed.

cifsChangeNotifyEnabled.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether a CIFS change notification is enabled for the FlexCache volume.

WeeklySchedule.description: Make a snapshot every week e.g. at Monday 04:00, Wednesday 05:20, Sunday 23:50

snapshotsToKeep.description: The maximum number of Snapshots to keep for the hourly schedule

minute.description: Set the minute of the hour to start the snapshot (0-59), defaults to the top of the hour (0).

hour.description: Set the hour to start the snapshot (0-23), defaults to midnight (0).

day.description: Set the day or days of the week to make a snapshot. Accepts a comma separated days of the week. Defaults to 'Sunday'.

QuotaRule.description: QuotaRule specifies the maximum disk space a user or group can use within a volume. They can be used for creating default and individual quota rules.

target.description: Optional. The quota rule applies to the specified user or group, identified by a Unix UID/GID, Windows SID, or null for default.

description.description: Optional. Description of the quota rule

state.description: Output only. State of the quota rule
enumDescriptions: Quota rule is in error state.

labels.description: Optional. Labels of the quota rule

diskLimitMib.description: Required. The maximum allowed disk space in MiB.

stateDetails.description: Output only. State details of the quota rule

createTime.description: Output only. Create time of the quota rule

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of the quota rule. Format: `projects/{project_number}/locations/{location_id}/volumes/volumes/{volume_id}/quotaRules/{quota_rule_id}`.

type.description: Required. The type of quota rule.

ListReplicationsResponse.description: ListReplicationsResponse is the result of ListReplicationsRequest.

replications.description: A list of replications in the project for the specified volume.

nextPageToken.description: The token you can use to retrieve the next page of results. Not returned if there are no more results in the list.

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

updateTime.description: Time when progress was updated last.

transferBytes.description: Cumulative bytes transferred so far for the replication relationship.

totalTransferDuration.description: Cumulative time taken across all transfers for the replication relationship.

lastTransferError.description: A message describing the cause of the last transfer failure.

lagDuration.description: Lag duration indicates the duration by which Destination region volume content lags behind the primary region volume content.

TransferStats.description: TransferStats reports all statistics related to replication transfer.

enumDescriptions: Backup is being created. While in this state, the snapshot for the backup point-in-time may not have been created yet, and so the point-in-time may not have been fixed.
Backup is being uploaded. While in this state, none of the writes to the volume will be included in the backup.
Backup is not valid and cannot be used for creating new volumes or restoring existing volumes.

backupRegion.description: Output only. Region in which backup is stored. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}`

volumeUsageBytes.description: Output only. Size of the file system when the backup was created. When creating a new volume from the backup, the volume capacity will have to be at least as big.

enforcedRetentionEndTime.description: Output only. The time until which the backup is not deletable.

createTime.description: Output only. The time when the backup was created.

labels.description: Resource labels to represent user provided metadata.

volumeRegion.description: Output only. Region of the volume from which the backup was created. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}`

description.description: A description of the backup with 2048 characters or less. Requests with longer descriptions will be rejected.

backupType.description: Output only. Type of backup, manually created or created by a backup policy.

sourceVolume.description: The resource name of the volume that this backup belongs to. You must provide either `source_volume` or `ontap_source`. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}`

sourceSnapshot.description: If specified, backup will be created from the given snapshot. If not specified, there will be a new snapshot taken to initiate the backup creation. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume_id}/snapshots/{snapshot_id}`

chainStorageBytes.description: Output only. Total size of all backups in a chain in bytes = baseline backup size + sum(incremental backup size)

name.description: Identifier. The resource name of the backup. Format: `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location}/backupVaults/{backup_vault_id}/backups/{backup_id}`.

satisfiesPzi.description: Output only. Reserved for future use

enabled.description: If enabled, make snapshots automatically according to the schedules. Default is false.

snapshotsToKeep.description: The maximum number of Snapshots to keep for the hourly schedule

minute.description: Set the minute of the hour to start the snapshot (0-59), defaults to the top of the hour (0).

HourlySchedule.description: Make a snapshot every hour e.g. at 04:00, 05:00, 06:00.

encryptDcConnections.description: If enabled, traffic between the SMB server to Domain Controller (DC) will be encrypted.

site.description: The Active Directory site the service will limit Domain Controller discovery too.

aesEncryption.description: If enabled, AES encryption will be enabled for SMB communication.

hour.description: Set the hour to start the snapshot (0-23), defaults to midnight (0).

unreachable.description: Locations that could not be reached.

weeklyBackupLimit.description: Number of weekly backups to keep. Note that the sum of daily, weekly and monthly backups should be greater than 1.

dailyBackupLimit.description: Number of daily backups to keep. Note that the minimum daily backup limit is 2.

monthlyBackupLimit.description: Number of monthly backups to keep. Note that the sum of daily, weekly and monthly backups should be greater than 1.

LargeCapacityConfig.description: Configuration for a Large Capacity Volume. A Large Capacity Volume supports sizes ranging from 4.8 TiB to 20 PiB; it is composed of multiple internal constituents, and must be created in a large capacity pool.

constituentCount.description: Optional. The number of internal constituents (e.g., FlexVols) for this large volume. The minimum number of constituents is 2.

name.description: The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the `name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

metadata.description: Service-specific metadata associated with the operation. It typically contains progress information and common metadata such as create time. Some services might not provide such metadata. Any method that returns a long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

response.description: The normal, successful response of the operation. If the original method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is `google.protobuf.Empty`. If the original method is standard `Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource. For other methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx` is the original method name. For example, if the original method name is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is `TakeSnapshotResponse`.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

done.description: If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress. If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is available.

Operation.description: This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a network API call.

sourceSnapshot.description: Output only. Specifies the full resource name of the source snapshot from which this volume was cloned. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume}/snapshots/{snapshot}

sourceVolume.description: Output only. Full name of the source volume resource. Format: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/volumes/{volume}

sharedSpaceGib.description: Output only. Shared space in GiB. Determined at volume creation time based on size of source snapshot.

UserCommands.description: UserCommands contains the commands to be executed by the customer.

commands.description: Output only. List of commands to be executed by the customer.

SimpleExportPolicyRule.description: An export policy rule describing various export options.

allowedClients.description: Comma separated list of allowed clients IP addresses

kerberos5pReadWrite.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines read and write access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'privacy' kerberos security mode. The 'kerberos5pReadOnly' value be ignored if this is enabled.

hasRootAccess.description: Whether Unix root access will be granted.

kerberos5ReadWrite.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines read and write access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'authentication' kerberos security mode. The 'kerberos5ReadOnly' value be ignored if this is enabled.

squashMode.description: Optional. Defines how user identity squashing is applied for this export rule. This field is the preferred way to configure squashing behavior and takes precedence over `has_root_access` if both are provided.
enumDescriptions: The root user (UID 0) retains full access. Other users are unaffected.
The root user (UID 0) is squashed to anonymous user ID. Other users are unaffected.
All users are squashed to anonymous user ID.

kerberos5iReadWrite.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines read and write access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'integrity' kerberos security mode. The 'kerberos5iReadOnly' value be ignored if this is enabled.

kerberos5pReadOnly.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines a read only access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'privacy' kerberos security mode.

anonUid.description: Optional. An integer representing the anonymous user ID. Range is 0 to `4294967295`. Required when `squash_mode` is `ROOT_SQUASH` or `ALL_SQUASH`.

kerberos5iReadOnly.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines a read only access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'integrity' kerberos security mode.

kerberos5ReadOnly.description: If enabled (true) the rule defines a read only access for clients matching the 'allowedClients' specification. It enables nfs clients to mount using 'authentication' kerberos security mode.

cancel.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.

filter.description: A filter to narrow down results to a preferred subset. The filtering language accepts strings like `"displayName=tokyo"`, and is documented in more detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).

pageSize.description: The maximum number of results to return. If not set, the service selects a default.

pageToken.description: A page token received from the `next_page_token` field in the response. Send that page token to receive the subsequent page.

extraLocationTypes.description: Optional. Do not use this field unless explicitly documented otherwise. This is primarily for internal usage.

list.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service. This method lists locations based on the resource scope provided in the ListLocationsRequest.name field: * **Global locations**: If `name` is empty, the method lists the public locations available to all projects. * **Project-specific locations**: If `name` follows the format `projects/{project}`, the method lists locations visible to that specific project. This includes public, private, or other project-specific locations enabled for the project. For gRPC and client library implementations, the resource name is passed as the `name` field. For direct service calls, the resource name is incorporated into the request path based on the specific service implementation and version.

name.description: Resource name for the location.
